notes: ProjectConfig (committed workflow) and LocalConfig (git-ignored active+defaults) both modeled.
EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile/ResolveEffectiveWorkflow layer project config under the task override at read time; no copying into task files.
When DiscoverTPDir returns empty, resolution reduces to built-in defaults (v0.23.0 behavior); every command guards on tpDir=="".
tp validate --project scans project task files and reports per-file workflow deviations from the project config.
Active pointer folded into .tp/local.json; setActiveFile no longer writes .tp-active; discover.go keeps legacy read as deprecated fallback removed in v0.25.0.
ProjectConfig has the single workflow key; doc-comment states it MUST be committed.
LocalConfig holds active and defaults; doc-comment states it MUST NOT be committed.
EnsureTPGitignore idempotently creates/appends local.json; called by WriteProjectConfig and WriteLocalConfig, which every .tp/ write goes through.
LoadProjectConfig/LoadLocalConfig return an empty struct on a missing file (equivalent to {}); files are independent.
DiscoverTPDir walks up from start testing each ancestor including anchor and boundary dir, stopping at first .tp/. Not memoized but deterministic from the single CWD anchor.
Walk halts after testing the git-boundary dir (hasGitBoundary matches .git dir or file) or filesystem root; never reads .tp/ above the boundary.
ProjectRoot/DiscoverTPDir always anchor at start (callers pass CWD "."), never re-anchored to --file.
No .tp/ discovered -> built-in defaults and per-task settings as in v0.23.0.
ProjectRoot returns dir containing .tp/, else git boundary, else CWD.
config.json parsed for single top-level workflow key; knownWorkflowKeys covers the seven workflow fields.
local.json parsed for active (string) and defaults (object of booleans).

Unknown top-level/workflow/defaults keys are collected as warnings and ignored, but only tp config surfaces config.json warnings on stderr; set/use/validate loaders drop them and no command surfaces local.json warnings.

Type-mismatch fallback-to-unset is implemented and returned as warnings, but most reading commands (set --project/--local, tp use, best-effort resolvers) drop the warnings; only tp config surfaces config.json ones.

clampWorkflowRanges falls back out-of-range values to built-in defaults and returns a warning; tp config surfaces it (config.go 84-86) but tp validate/--project does not read config warnings, so the spec's 'tp validate warns' half is unmet.
MalformedConfigError (unreadable/invalid-JSON/non-object incl empty/whitespace) aborts with exit 3 and a 'repair or delete <path>' hint; empty object {} is valid; config/set/use surface it.
output.JSON writes stdout; Info/Error/Success write stderr, so surfaced config warnings never pollute stdout JSON.
Rank-1 CLI flags --file/--compact/--no-compact/--color/--no-color registered; applyFlagDefaults gives explicit flags top precedence.
TP_FILE resolves active at rank 2; NO_COLOR handled below explicit flags in flag_defaults.go 51-62 (CLI overrides its env peer).
Task-file workflow override is the highest layer in ResolveWorkflowLayers (rank 3, workflow fields only).
Project config workflow (rank 4) sits under the task override; local defaults applied at rank 4 in flag_defaults.go.
DefaultWorkflow supplies the rank-5 built-in defaults.
Per-field sparse merge via pickInt/pickString/pickChecks: a task file setting only one field inherits the rest from project.
Workflow fields resolve across override/project/default only; no_color has NO_COLOR env peer; compact/quiet resolve CLI/local/default with no env layer.
active is resolved by the DiscoverTaskFile discovery chain, not the workflow ladder.
WorkflowOverride uses pointer fields (and *[]Check) so presence is tracked and review_max_rounds:0 stays distinct from absent.
pickInt/pickString return the first non-nil layer: a present key overrides regardless of value; absent inherits.
pickChecks returns the first non-nil *[]Check (replace semantics), so an explicit empty checks array replaces project checks and only an absent key inherits.
set --workflow --project writes the workflow block of .tp/config.json via ProjectConfigDir (git boundary or CWD), creating .tp/ and .gitignore.
Out-of-range integer writes rejected via workflowFieldRange (timeout 30-3600, clean 1-10, caps 0-50) with exit 1 (ExitValidation).
Project-level quality_gate is authorable (handled directly, not routed through readOnlyWorkflowFields).
set --local defaults.<flag>=<bool> restricted to compact/quiet/no_color and true/false (exit 1 otherwise); creates .tp/ and gitignore; writes under flock.
Import convergence (ResolveWorkflow), gate (EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile), and review/audit budgets (ResolveWorkflow in review_status.go/audit.go) all read layered resolved values.
A thinned task file inherits the project cap at enforcement and the project quality_gate at tp done (EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile layers project under override).
tp config prints resolved workflow JSON on stdout; a missing task file yields the project/default layer alone and exits 0.

resolvedConfig annotates only workflow fields with override/project/default; the spec's active and defaults {value,source} annotations (source cli/env/local/legacy/autodetect) are missing from --resolved output.
runConfig always emits the same JSON shape regardless of --compact (documented no-op).
local.json active (project-root-relative) sits in the chain --file > TP_FILE > active > .tp-active > auto-detect.
tp use <file> resolves to abs, stores project-root-relative in local.json active, rejects outside-root with exit 2 (ExitUsage), no longer writes .tp-active.
tp use --clear removes active from local.json (no-op exit 0 when unset) and also removes a leftover legacy .tp-active.
tp use with no argument prints the resolved active file and the discovery-chain rank (cli/env/local/legacy/autodetect).

A missing active target warns on stderr and continues down the chain (no abort), but tp use with no arg does not report the dangling pointer: resolvedActiveSource (use.go 61-82) silently falls through to autodetect on a failed stat.
An absolute or escaping active value is rejected with a stderr warning and treated as unset (returns "").
Legacy .tp-active read from CWD only as a lower-priority fallback with a deprecation warning naming the tp use migration path.
local.json active is checked before .tp-active, so the project-local value wins when both exist and the target resolves.
Deprecation warning states .tp-active is removed in v0.25.0; still honored through v0.24.x.
local.json defaults supply compact/quiet/no_color defaults applied only when the flag is absent; --json is excluded (never applied).
Negating flags --no-compact/--no-quiet/--color registered (root.go 50-52) and force the boolean off, so a default is overridable both directions; explicit flag wins.
no_color resolves in order: explicit --color/--no-color, NO_COLOR env, defaults.no_color, then TTY (downstream fatih/color).
Passing a flag and its negation together is a usage error (exit 2) via the conflict() checks.
workflowDeviations reports per-file each field where the override differs from a value the project explicitly sets, naming file/field/override/project.
Comparisons require both override and project non-nil, so a field the project does not set is not a deviation.
checksEqual is an order-insensitive multiset comparison over {class,cmd}; a checks deviation is reported as N vs M entries, not a scalar.
Deviations are exit 0 by default; --strict with deviations exits 1 (ExitValidation).
No .tp/config.json -> reports project_config:false and exits 0.
ScanProjectTaskFiles collects every *.tasks.json from the project root downward; used by both validate --project and config --extract.
Recursion skips .git/.tp/node_modules/vendor and SkipDirs any nested subdir containing its own .git (submodule).
ScanProjectTaskFiles is a distinct recursive scan, separate from DiscoverTaskFile/findTaskFiles single-file auto-detect, used only by the two project-scoped commands.
validate --project warns on stderr and skips a malformed task file; config --extract aborts with exit 3 (config_extract.go 148-162).
With no .tp/, resolution reduces to v0.23.0; no migration is performed on upgrade.
Adoption is opt-in via hand-editing or the extractor; no code rewrites a repo automatically on upgrade.
computeCommonPolicy hoists a field (incl quality_gate string and checks by set equality) only when every file sets it identically; StripTaskWorkflowFields then removes hoisted fields from each file.
--dry-run prints the plan without writing; dirty tree -> exit 4; outside git skips+warns; existing config -> exit 4 unless --force merges.
Full task-file workflow blocks parse into presence-tracked overrides (LoadTaskWorkflowOverride), so every explicit field remains an override; model.Workflow unmarshal keeps existing files working.
ResolveWorkflowLayers is exactly two layers (task override + project) over built-in defaults; no N-level cascade.
Project config is anchored once at CWD (projectWorkflowOverride(".")); a --file into another project still resolves against the CWD project config.
Resolution has only project + built-in layers; no user-home/global (~/.config/tp) config code exists.
v0.24.0 only layers config into workflow resolution; task/coverage/review/audit semantics are unchanged.
ProjectConfig/LocalConfig/WorkflowOverride defined with commit/no-commit contracts; absent==empty via nil fields; covered by model/projectconfig_test.go.
DiscoverTPDir walks upward including anchor+boundary, halts at .git dir-or-file boundary; covered by projectconfig_test.go TestDiscoverTPDir_* (git-as-file, halts-at-boundary).
ProjectRoot returns .tp/ parent, else git boundary, else CWD; anchored at start, never at --file; covered by TestProjectRoot_*.
EnsureTPGitignore idempotently creates/appends local.json entry; covered by TestEnsureTPGitignore_CreatesWhenAbsent/IdempotentAppend.

parseWorkflowOverride/LoadProjectConfig/LoadLocalConfig parse the schema with pointer presence-tracking; covered by TestLoadProjectConfig_ParsesWorkflowWithPresence and TestLoadLocalConfig_ParsesActiveAndDefaults.

Unknown keys ignored+warned and type-mismatched values fall back to unset; covered by TestLoadProjectConfig_UnknownKeysWarn/TypeMismatchFallsBack. Note: on-stderr surfacing only via tp config (see list-3-3/3-4).

Range fallback to built-in defaults implemented and tested (TestLoadProjectConfig_OutOfRangeFallsBack); tp config surfaces the warning but tp validate does not read config warnings, so the acceptance's 'tp validate warns on stderr' half is unmet.

MalformedConfigError aborts config/set/use with exit 3 + repair-or-delete hint; empty object valid; covered by TestLoadProjectConfig_MalformedAborts/EmptyObjectIsValid. Note: best-effort resolvers (gate/budget/import) swallow malformed config rather than aborting.
ResolveWorkflowLayers plus flag_defaults implement the CLI>env>override>project>default ladder; active resolved by the discovery chain; covered by configresolve_test.go.
Per-field sparse merge; flag_defaults enforces that no_color has the NO_COLOR env peer while compact/quiet have none; covered by configresolve_test.go/flag_defaults_test.go.
Presence (pointer non-nil) defines an override incl the 0 value; pickChecks gives checks replace semantics incl explicit empty array.
Exactly two workflow layers (project + per-task) anchored once at CWD; no cross-project/monorepo re-anchoring.

enforceImportConvergence reads ResolveWorkflow (project layered under override, workflow_resolve.go 27-47), so a thinned file inherits the project clean-rounds/cap; covered by import_workflow_test.go/import_enforce_test.go.
executeQualityGate/runQualityGatePreFlock use EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile, so a task file omitting quality_gate runs the project gate; covered by gate_test.go/donegate_test.go.
Review and audit budget checks call engine.ResolveWorkflow (review_status.go 27, audit.go 217, audit_record.go 33-38/150-172), inheriting project caps; covered by budget_test.go.
tp config prints resolved workflow JSON; missing task file yields project/default layer alone at exit 0; --compact is a no-op; command registered in root.go 137; covered by config_test.go.

--resolved annotates workflow fields with override/project/default correctly, but does not emit the active or defaults {value,source} annotations (source enum cli/env/local/legacy/autodetect) required by spec section 6.2.
Writes the workflow block of .tp/config.json via ProjectConfigDir; creates .tp/ + .gitignore at git boundary or CWD; writes under flock (WithFileLock).
Same out-of-range rejection as per-task (workflowFieldRange, exit 1); project quality_gate is authorable; covered by set_project_test.go.
Writes defaults into .tp/local.json, creating .tp/ + .gitignore + local.json in the same location as --project; under flock; covered by set_local_test.go.
Flag restricted to compact/quiet/no_color and value to true/false; unknown flag or non-boolean rejected with exit 1; covered by set_local_test.go.
local.json active (project-root-relative via ResolveLocalActive) sits at chain rank --file>TP_FILE>active>.tp-active>auto-detect; covered by discover_test.go/use_active_test.go.
setActiveFile resolves to abs, stores project-root-relative, rejects outside-root with exit 2, creates .tp/ files, no .tp-active written.
clearActiveFile removes active from local.json (no-op exit 0 when unset) and removes leftover legacy .tp-active; covered by use_active_test.go.
No-argument tp use prints resolved active file and discovery-chain rank; folds active pointer into local.json with v0.25.0 window.

Dangling active warns on stderr and continues to .tp-active/auto-detect; escape/absolute rejection implemented (configresolve.go 31-41). But tp use with no arg does not report the dangling pointer (use.go 61-82 silently falls through to autodetect).
Legacy .tp-active read from CWD only, deprecation warning names tp use migration path; project-local wins when both exist; removal stated for v0.25.0.
applyFlagDefaults (invoked in root.go PersistentPreRun) applies compact/quiet/no_color from LocalFlagDefaults when the flag is absent; --json excluded; covered by flag_defaults_test.go.
--no-compact/--no-quiet/--color registered (root.go 50-52) override defaults both directions; explicit wins; flag+negation together is exit 2; covered by flag_defaults_test.go.
no_color order: explicit flag, NO_COLOR env, defaults.no_color, TTY; project default below env/flags; covered by flag_defaults_test.go.
Shared recursive scan from project root collects every *.tasks.json in root and subdirs; used by validate --project and config --extract only; covered by scan_test.go.
Skips .git/.tp/node_modules/vendor and nested-submodule dirs; malformed file skipped by validate --project (warn stderr) but aborts config --extract with exit 3 (config_extract.go 148-162).
Reports per-file override-vs-project deviations naming file/field/override/project; a field the project does not set is not a deviation; covered by validate_project_test.go.
checksEqual is order-insensitive multiset comparison over {class,cmd}; checks deviation reported as N vs M entries; covered by validate_project_test.go.
Deviations exit 0 by default; --strict with deviations exits 1; no config -> reports and exits 0; covered by validate_project_test.go.

Hoists a field into config.json only when every file sets it identically (checks by set equality; quality_gate string); StripTaskWorkflowFields removes hoisted fields from each file; covered by config_extract_test.go.
No common field -> writes nothing, reports nothing-to-hoist, exit 0; malformed task file mid-scan aborts with exit 3; covered by config_extract_test.go.
--dry-run prints plan without writing; without it, dirty git tree (git status --porcelain) refuses with exit 4; outside git skips the clean-tree check and warns.

Existing config refused with exit 4 unless --force, which mergeCommon overwrites only hoisted keys preserving other project fields; dirty-tree gate runs before and is never bypassed by --force; writes under flock (WriteProjectConfig).
No-.tp/ path reduces to v0.23.0 defaults; existing task files and .tp-active keep working; engine/backcompat_test.go exercises zero-config behavior.
Full workflow blocks parse as overrides (LoadTaskWorkflowOverride); nothing is auto-rewritten; thin-then-resolve works via layering; covered by engine/backcompat_test.go and workflow_resolve_test.go.
Resolution keeps a single project layer under CLI/env with no user-home config; review/audit/coverage semantics unchanged (only the config layer added).

git status runs via exec.Command arg slice (L130), no shell/injection; writes 0o600. But WriteProjectConfig (L200) and StripTaskWorkflowFields (L206) rewrite config.json and task files WITHOUT WithFileLock, unlike set_project which flocks the same config.json — concurrent writers can race. Also L198 pc,_,_:=LoadProjectConfig swallows MalformedConfigError on --force merge, silently overwriting a corrupt config.

Gate runs via engine.RunCommand -> sh -c (runcmd.go L72). Project-layer quality_gate is now authorable (set_project / committed .tp/config.json), so cloning a repo and running tp done executes its configured shell command — inherent to the feature, same surface as the v0.23 task-file gate. json.Marshal errors ignored at L85/L163 are benign (infallible map). Gate runs pre-flock by design; no lock/write here.

Read-only enforcement; no exec/lock/write. ResolveWorkflow's second return (L34) is the source label, not an error — nothing swallowed. A malformed .tp/config.json resolves best-effort to conservative defaults (review_clean_rounds=2), so enforcement stays strict and is never weakened by config corruption.
CLI wiring only: flag registration, NO_COLOR env read (L143), command assembly. No locks, exec, file writes, or untrusted path construction.

Strong input validation: flag must be compact/quiet/no_color (L42), value must be true/false (L47-56). Write under WithFileLock (L65); config 0o600, dir 0o755. os.Exit inside the flock callback (L74) skips the deferred Unlock, but process exit releases the OS advisory lock and leaves only a harmless empty .lock — established codebase pattern.

flock (L82) + 0o600 writes and range-checked ints are sound; checks validated via ValidateChecks; quality_gate arbitrary string is by design. Weakness: fmt.Sscanf(valueStr,%d,&val) at L60 accepts trailing garbage (gate_timeout_seconds=100x stored as 100). Non-integer (L61) and unknown-field (L71) paths return a JSON error with exit 0 instead of ExitValidation — inconsistent with the L64-67 range error's exit 1.

setActiveFile validates the target does not escape the project root via filepath.Rel + HasPrefix("..") (L146-151) after Abs+Stat; stores a project-root-relative path. Writes under WithFileLock (L158), config 0o600. os.Exit-in-flock is the benign process-exit-releases-lock pattern.
Read-only validation command; no locks/exec/writes. DiscoverTaskFile and output.JSON errors are checked and exit properly (L34, L50-53).

Read-only advisory report; no locks/exec/writes. files,_:=engine.ScanProjectTaskFiles(root) at L75 swallows the walk error, so a scan failure silently under-reports deviations. Low impact (advisory, exit 0 unless --strict). filepath.Rel errors at L82/L87 are benign (both paths absolute under root).

Active-pointer defense is sound: absolute paths rejected (L31), and filepath.Join+Rel+HasPrefix("..") rejects escapes (L37-40) — no traversal bypass. Ding: EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (L141) override,_:=LoadTaskWorkflowOverride swallows the read/parse error, so a malformed task-file workflow block is silently ignored and the gate resolves over project/default. Documented best-effort, but an ignored error return.

All file writes use 0o600 (L229 StripTaskWorkflowFields, L255 config.json, L271 local.json, L285/L299 .gitignore); dirs 0o755. Malformed/unreadable config surfaced as MalformedConfigError with a repair-or-delete hint (L124/L128/L157/L161), not swallowed. Write primitives intentionally do not self-lock (caller responsibility); set_* callers flock — config_extract does not (flagged under file-sec-0).

All helpers unexported and carry doc comments (sourceLabel, resolvedConfig, resolveConfigWorkflow); errors.As routes MalformedConfigError to a hinted ExitFile; funcs well under 80 lines; gofmt-clean; no TODO/FIXME or dead code.

overrides preallocated via make([]WorkflowOverride,0,n) (L147); empty hoist emits []string{} not nil (L168); %v used only on terminal os.Exit paths, nothing propagated so no %w needed; every func documented; runConfigExtract 76 lines; gofmt-clean.
Single documented func applyFlagDefaults (46 lines); precedence logic clear; conflicts exit ExitUsage(2); no propagated errors, no slices for JSON; camelCase naming; gofmt-clean; no TODO.

gofmt -l flags gate.go: trailing blank line at EOF (L196); gofmt -w removes it. Slips past the quality gate because .golangci.yml enables no gofmt/gofumpt formatter. Content otherwise clean: every func documented, all under 80 lines, consistent Exit* codes, no dead code.

Single documented func (38 lines); LoadReviewState error routed to exitStateError (L23); ResolveWorkflow error deliberately discarded via _ (best-effort, defaults apply, L34); ExitValidation used consistently; gofmt-clean; no TODO.

gofmt -l flags root.go: var-block misalignment, flagFile needs 2 more spaces (L16); gofmt -w fixes. Separately, 7 exported symbols lack doc comments (NewRootCmd, Execute, GetFileFlag/IsJSONOutput/IsQuiet/IsNoColor/IsCompact, L142-158), violating the exported-doc-comment rule. Neither is caught by the gate (no gofmt formatter enabled).

runSetLocal 68 lines and documented; localDefaultFlags documented; errors.As for MalformedConfigError (L68); lc.Defaults nil-map guarded (L77); write flock-wrapped; validation failures exit ExitValidation(1)/ExitUsage(2) consistently; gofmt-clean.

Validation failures use inconsistent exit codes: non-integer value (L61) and unknown field (L71) return output.JSON to stdout -> exit 0, while out-of-range (L64-66) os.Exit(ExitValidation)=1. Exit-0-on-validation-error defeats an agent's exit-code check (documented 1=validation). Faithfully mirrors pre-existing set.go (L294/L320), so not a v0.24.0 regression. Also runSetProjectWorkflow is ~108 lines (L20-128, incl. flock closure), over the ~80 guideline.

Doc block above resolvedActiveSource (L55-60) opens with a stale leftover paragraph describing showActiveFile (L55-57), while showActiveFile itself (L84) has no doc comment. Go doc comments should start with and immediately precede the symbol; a refactor left showActiveFile's comment orphaned above the wrong function. Logic is otherwise sound.

runValidate/newValidateCmd unexported (no doc-comment obligation); proper ExitFile(3)/ExitValidation(1) exits; cleanly delegates --project to runValidateProject; under 80 lines; gofmt-clean; no TODO/dead code.
